task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI is set to Playback mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'PLAYBACK' option is selected in the OpenBCI GUI System Control Panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct recording file is selected for playback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is visible in the file selection area of the System Control Panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that the EEG data playback is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: EEG data traces are visible and scrolling in the Time Series widget, or the system control panel shows 'Stop Data Stream' indicating it is currently running.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Networking widget is open and configured for the LSL protocol.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Networking widget is visible in one of the GUI panels, and 'LSL' is selected as the active protocol.

task_completion.description: Verify that the LSL stream name is set to the custom target name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'OpenBCI_Station_A' is entered into the Stream Name (or equivalent) text field within the Networking widget's LSL configuration.

task_completion.description: Verify that the LSL broadcast has been started.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start' button in the Networking widget has been clicked (often changing to 'Stop' or showing an active/broadcasting status indicator next to the LSL configuration).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible, and the configurations are made within its interface, not in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use a terminal script to mock the LSL stream.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows running Python scripts (e.g., using pylsl directly) to fake the LSL broadcast.

integrity.description: Verify that the recording file was loaded through the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path 'Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is seen in the GUI's file explorer or system control panel, not just typed into a separate document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target LSL stream name must be exactly 'OpenBCI_Station_A' and the stream type is 'EEG' (TimeSeries data broadcasts as EEG by default). The playback file is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'. The protocol must be LSL. Verification relies on seeing these exact names and settings actively running in the OpenBCI GUI.
